items.headline: Clippers loss to Warriors means the rich get richer
items.blurb: Clippers loss to Warriors means the rich get richer — The Los Angeles Clippers will miss the NBA playoffs after a 125-120 loss to the Golden State Warriors. That defeat will impact a lot more teams than just the Clips.

items.blurb: ‘He just has to be himself’: Darko on how Scottie can be most impactful — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajaković says Scottie Barnes continues to be underappreciated for his play, adding that simply being himself and maintaining his strong performance is all the team can ask for in the playoffs.

items.headline: Warriors elder statesmen's playoff experience helps stun Clippers in comeback win
items.blurb: Warriors elder statesmen's playoff experience helps stun Clippers in comeback win — The Golden State Warriors had to rely on 36-year-old Draymond Green, 39-year-old Al Horford and a hobbled 38-year-old Steph Curry. Down the stretch, the trio showed their playoff pedigree to shock the Los Angeles Clippers.

items.headline: At 37, Rockets superstar Kevin Durant still belongs on All-NBA first team
items.blurb: At 37, Rockets superstar Kevin Durant still belongs on All-NBA first team — At 37, Kevin Durant continues to defy time. In his first season with the Houston Rockets, the veteran forward delivered another elite campaign, proving he remains one of the most unstoppable scorers in NBA history.

items.blurb: Steph Curry reacts to Draymond Green's impact in win over Clippers — The Golden State Warriors' dynasty might be fading, but they're not going down without a fight. Draymond Green and Stephen Curry turned back the clock to take down the Los Angeles Clippers on the road.

items.blurb: Wizards Retain Brian Keefe As Head Coach For 2026-27 Season — Washington Wizards general manager Will Dawkins confirmed Thursday that head coach Brian Keefe will return for the 2026-27 season, transitioning the franchise from a deliberate rebuild toward a competitive push led by Trae Young and Anthony Davis. items.blurb: Joel Embiid: Likely to miss start of playoffs — ESPN's Shams Charania reported Thursday on NBA Today that Embiid (abdomen) isn't expected to be available for the start of the playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: How a driven Ja’Kobe Walter earned crucial role with Raptors — For Ja’Kobe Walter, the ability to ‘figure it out’ was baked in. Michael Grange has the story on how the second-year wing earned a crucial role on the playoff-bound Raptors.

items.blurb: Ja Morant Has Informed Players, Former Coaches He's Done Playing For Grizzlies — Ja Morant has privately told players around the league and some of his former coaches that he is no longer willing to play for the Memphis Grizzlies, multiple league sources told ESPN. The breaking point traces directly to a one-game suspension Morant received early in the season following a locker room confrontation
